Marc Andreessen believes startups burning cash--but they'll still funding.
Startups might spending responsibly, that doesn't mean venture capitalists will giving them less money.
fact, they might spending more.
"The argument that tech permeating more industries than ever before," said venture capitalist Marc Andreessen Wall Street Journal Digital conference Laguna Beach, Calif.
Last month, Andreessen made headlines Tweetstorm critiquing tech startups blowing through cash, telling them "worry" about their spending.
Related: Apple signup "fantastic"
held firm stance burn rates (how quickly firms spend capital) conversation with Jonathan Krim Wall Street Journal Tuesday.
"Cash burn getting hand," said Andreessen.
"You want have reasonable valuation, reasonable cash burn."
said founders often overly excited depressed) spent money quickly.
That said, venture capital funding "exercise" finding unicorns, according Andreessen.
Venture capitalists invest billion about 4,000 companies year, knowing that only about will home runs.
"Every months, there companies created that will generate [venture capital] returns," Andreessen said.
with technology touching more industries will that number increase?
"That's debate we're having internally," said Andreessen.
"Airbnb's [valuation] hard argue with."
Airbnb valued billion earlier this year, Andreessen called "home run" real estate startups.
said Uber Lyft which investor) were same transportation industry.
